content_block.content: Hybrid Vietnam Itinerary (4–11 Apr 2026)

**Optimized 8‑day itinerary** blending the **geographic efficiency** of the Easter plan (Da Nang → Hue → Hoi An → Saigon) with the **concrete logistics, swimming schedule, and safety details** from the expert recommendation. Focus remains on **culture, handicrafts, local life, natural exploration, and history**.
🗺️ Route & Flight Details

**Route:** Hong Kong → Da Nang → Hue → Hoi An → Ho Chi Minh City → Hong Kong (no backtracking)
• **Outbound:** UO552 (08:25 HKG→DAD) – arrive early 4 Apr
• **Domestic:** Da Nang → Ho Chi Minh City (VietJet/Vietnam Airlines, ~1.5 h) – evening of 10 Apr
• **Return:** VJ876 (15:10 SGN→HKG) – 11 Apr
• **E‑visa:** Select Da Nang (DAD) as entry, Ho Chi Minh (SGN) as exit; apply now (3–5 working days).
**Day 1 (4 Apr, Sat) – Arrival in Da Nang & My Khe Beach**

• **08:25** – Fly HKG→DAD (UO552)
• **09:20** – Arrive Da Nang; e‑visa entry; Grab to hotel (My Khe Beach area)
• **Afternoon** – Check‑in, lunch at seafood restaurant
• **Late afternoon** – First swim at My Khe Beach (water ~28 °C)
• **Evening** – Beach sunset; Dragon Bridge fire/water show (weekends, 21:00)
**Day 2 (5 Apr, Sun) – Da Nang: Marble Mountains & Stone‑Carving Village**

• **Morning** – Swim at My Khe Beach (06:00–07:30)
• **Late morning** – Marble Mountains (Thuy Son) – cave temples, elevator to summit
• **Afternoon** – Non Nuoc Stone‑Carving Village – 300 workshops, marble souvenirs (Buddha statues, zodiac figurines)
• **Late afternoon** – Da Nang Cathedral (Pink Church) photo stop
• **Evening** – Vietnamese seafood dinner along My Khe Beach
**Day 3 (6 Apr, Mon) – Travel to Hue & Imperial Grandeur**

• **Morning** – Scenic transfer to Hue via Hai Van Pass (stop at Lap An Lagoon, Lăng Cô Beach)
• **Afternoon** – Hue Imperial City (UNESCO) – guided walk through Forbidden Purple City, Ngo Mon Gate, Thai Hoa Palace
• **Evening** – Boat tour on Perfume River, listen to traditional Hue singing
**Day 4 (7 Apr, Tue) – Hue’s Royal Tombs & Traditional Crafts**

• **Morning** – Nguyen Dynasty Royal Tombs along Perfume River – Minh Mang, Tu Duc
• **Afternoon** – Craft villages – Thuy Xuan Incense Village (make your own incense), lotus‑leaf conical‑hat workshops, rice‑paper making
• **Evening** – Panoramic view from Vong Canh Hill
**Day 5 (8 Apr, Wed) – Travel to Hoi An & Ancient Town Charm**

• **Morning** – Bus transfer from Hue to Hoi An (3–4 h) with stops at Hai Van Pass, Lap An Lagoon
• **Afternoon** – Check‑in near Ancient Town; walking tour (Japanese Covered Bridge, Phuc Kien Assembly Hall, Tan Ky Old House)
• **Evening** – Swim at An Bang Beach (quieter than My Khe)
• **Night** – Lantern‑making workshop, dinner in lantern‑lit streets
**Day 6 (9 Apr, Thu) – Hoi An’s Handicrafts & Countryside Life**

• **Morning** – Swim at An Bang Beach; explore Hoi An Ancient Town craft street
• **Lunch** – Must‑try Cao Lau + White Rose dumplings
• **Afternoon** – Thanh Ha Pottery Village (500‑year‑old) – hand‑make couple‑mugs
• **Late afternoon** – Tra Que Vegetable Village – organic farming activities, cooking class with fresh herbs
• **Evening** – Swim at Chu Dai Beach (10 min from town)
• **Night** – Night market shopping & street food
**Day 7 (10 Apr, Fri) – Hoi An Free Morning → Fly to Ho Chi Minh City**

• **Morning** – Free time in Hoi An (optional basket‑boat ride in Cam Thanh coconut forest, crab‑catching with local fishermen)
• **Afternoon** – Transfer to Da Nang Airport (~40 min)
• **Evening** – Fly DAD→SGN (~1.5 h); check‑in District 1
• **Night** – Light dinner, first impressions of Saigon
**Day 8 (11 Apr, Sat) – Ho Chi Minh City Highlights → Return to Hong Kong**

• **Morning** – Central Post Office + Notre‑Dame Cathedral (exterior)
• **Afternoon** – Reunification Palace + War Remnants Museum
• **Late afternoon** – Ben Thanh Market – handicrafts, coffee, cashews
• **15:10** – Fly SGN→HKG (VJ876)
• **18:50** – Arrive Hong Kong
**⚠️ Safety note:** Ho Chi Minh City has high motorcycle density; keep phones/purses secure, cross streets steadily.
🏊 Swimming Opportunities

• **4 Apr (evening)** – My Khe Beach (first dip + sunset)
• **5 Apr (morning)** – My Khe Beach (06:00–07:30)
• **8 Apr (evening)** – An Bang Beach (quieter)
• **9 Apr (morning & evening)** – An Bang + Chu Dai Beach (main swimming day)

**Total:** 4–5 swims, comfortable pace.
🛍️ Handicraft Shopping List

• **Da Nang (Non Nuoc)** – Marble Buddha statues, zodiac figurines
• **Hue (craft villages)** – Incense sticks, conical hats, rice‑paper, court‑style souvenirs
• **Hoi An (Ancient Town)** – Silk lanterns, custom Áo Dài, wood carvings, lacquerware
• **Hoi An (Thanh Ha)** – Hand‑made pottery (couple mugs)
• **Ho Chi Minh City (Ben Thanh Market)** – Silk, lacquerware, coffee, cashews
⚠️ Important Reminders

• **Visa** – E‑visa (USD 25, 3–5 working days); select Da Nang (DAD) as entry, Ho Chi Minh (SGN) as exit; carry printed copy.
• **Safety** – Keep phones/bags secure in HCMC; use Grab (app‑based) for taxis; avoid casinos (recent warnings).
• **Emergency contacts** – Vietnam police 113; Consulate in Da Nang +84‑905‑580‑010; Consulate in HCMC +84‑908‑002‑226; Chinese MFA +86‑10‑12308.
📋 Immediate Next Steps

1. Apply for e‑visa (today/tomorrow)
2. Book HKG→DAD flight (UO552 recommended)
3. Book SGN→HKG return flight (VJ876 recommended)
4. Domestic DAD→SGN flight can be booked later (plenty of availability)
5. After flights confirmed, refine hotel & restaurant list.
